en_title: An Embrace for the Afterimage of an Old Beloved
en_body:
deeper, more drenched, overflowing than the rolling rivers
more ardent in intensity, more wildly rushing than waterfalls pouring down
higher, reaching farther than billions of star-years into the realm
Your-Love: is there and absolutely impassioned, more!
warming my two hands, I, soft-frail in the frozen night
singing my lashes a lullaby, gently trembling, into a little mystic hymn
so lightly dreaming: You water me drenched in love's enchantment
so softly intoxicated: You kiss me into wakefulness wildly drunk!
far, far at the horizon's complete turning,
forever unfolding, my feet run seeking the ultimate point:
to solve the proposition of breaking the invisible boundary;
immense, range upon range: the grasslands
Your homeland nurtures: mountain winds caress the carpet of grass
Your four seasons, serenely harmonized in legendary Freedom:
Your-Love: has embraced, cherished, struggled to lift..me!
as in the vow You spoke: forever and breaking through frontiers
Caressing the skin, the ivory-jade body of Five-Thousand-Years!
Your-Love: touches and strokes my night so tenderly
When I turn my face to look: Your eyes are like the Evening Star
Your lips, a thousand prayers of love and Grace
Your voice, warm-soft words of sustaining
Your questions, your counsels: true-hearted:
Carry me into a wondrous sleep:
There all creatures roam the Holy Land!
There children gather in flocks like wild lambs,
Through all four seasons only singing and laughing sweetly...
warm gold, delicious, the hearth-fire within wilderness,
in the frozen night of the wild country:
Your-gaze, blue as the sky of the old village,
radiant as vows that remain forever stars:
the promises ancient lovers gave as prayers!
within the tones of such passionate faithfulness,
Your arms forever burning, softly longing:
hold me tightly: lest tomorrow love be unfulfilled!
space and time like cycles of the universe
and we, the children with hair of two colors
the growing-wise along love's far journey
carries us drifting toward ten-thousand separating ways:
But, still remaining here, the afterimage of the beloved:
bearing within new anguish,
the restless turnings of another destiny!
sometimes sitting remembering You:
old beloved: distance has no meaning
but: separation within the color of the trembling heart
the pulse does not stir the skin of the heart
so thin...
may the road You travel be wide-open as the heart
You widen; Your vow, because of them, I remain:
I-alone..!!
note: “Five-Thousand-Years” invokes the ancient continuity of the Vietnamese homeland, while the Holy Land and lambs braid Biblical imagery into the beloved's refuge.
